evals.name: investor-update
evals.prompt: I write a GIX investor update once a quarter. It's chaotic — I draft something, Greg reviews, we go back and forth on numbers, I pull figures from the financial model, send it out. The quality varies a lot run to run. I want to spec this as a process so next time a Claude Code agent can do most of it without me starting from scratch. Walk me through designing it.
evals.expected_output: A process-spec markdown file with frontmatter, all required sections, a Mermaid flowchart showing the draft → review → revise → send loop with explicit gates, metrics across all four categories, and a build handoff for Claude Code.
expectations: Spec file written to the expected output path
verify_spec.py on the spec exits 0 (Gate 6 passes)
verify_spec.py --final on the spec exits 0 (all final blocking assertions pass)
verify_spec.py --final stdout includes structured 'PASSED: N/N final blocking assertions' summary line (count is proof-of-work evidence; matches between numerator and denominator)
Frontmatter has status: verified (Phase 6 verification + promotion completed)
Frontmatter has build_target: claude-code
Mermaid block has at least 5 step nodes plus at least one explicit GATE decision node
Procedure includes an explicit review/iteration loop (revisions go back to review)
Requirement Owners table names a real reviewer for the review step (e.g. Greg)
Inputs include the financial model as a named, controllable input
Output metric is testable — names a measurable property of the sent update, not 'output is correct'
Edge Cases section has at least 3 real (non-placeholder) rows
Build handoff file exists at outputs/build-handoff.md and references the spec path
Build handoff names Claude-Code-specific capture mechanisms (hooks, MCP servers)
Session notes describe each step that ran (Steps 1–7 always; Steps 0 and 8 only if their conditional triggers fired)

evals.name: weekly-review
evals.prompt: Every Friday I do a weekly review — what shipped, what's stuck, what to focus on next week. I do it intuitively, takes 30-60 min, and the quality is uneven. I'd like to make it measurable and consistent so I can spot when I'm drifting. Help me design this as a real process.
evals.expected_output: A process-spec markdown file for a periodic ritual: clear output (review artifact), inputs (week's activity, prior commitments), metrics that catch drift, and a build target hint of either manual or agent-supported.
expectations: Spec file written to the expected output path
verify_spec.py on the spec exits 0 (Gate 6 passes)
verify_spec.py --final on the spec exits 0 (all final blocking assertions pass)
verify_spec.py --final stdout includes structured 'PASSED: N/N final blocking assertions' summary line (count is proof-of-work evidence; matches between numerator and denominator)
Frontmatter has status: verified
Cadence is named (Friday or weekly) somewhere in the spec
Output is a written review artifact (markdown / document), not a verb
Inputs include both the week's completed activity AND prior-week commitments
Process Health or Output metrics include at least one drift-detection signal (e.g. % commitments completed, focus-area continuity score, week-over-week velocity)
Mermaid block has at least 5 step nodes plus at least one GATE decision node
Edge Cases include 'commitment slipped' or equivalent recurring failure mode
Build handoff file exists and references the chosen target with appropriate capture mechanisms
Decision rules resolve on input alone (no 'use judgment' or vague criteria)
Session notes describe each step that ran (Steps 1–7 always; Steps 0 and 8 only if their conditional triggers fired)

evals.name: drive-manager-meta
evals.prompt: I have a drive-manager skill that routes files from my Desktop Inbox to the right folder. Sometimes it routes wrong because it doesn't have enough context. I want to design the meta-process for: when a routing rule turns out wrong, how do I capture that learning and feed it back into the skill's rules? Spec this so I can build it as a Python script.
evals.expected_output: A process-spec markdown file for an exception-handling feedback loop: trigger (routing wrong), capture (what learning), feed back (where the rule lives, how it gets updated), with a Python-script build handoff.
expectations: Spec file written to the expected output path
verify_spec.py on the spec exits 0
verify_spec.py --final on the spec exits 0 (all final blocking assertions pass)
verify_spec.py --final stdout includes structured 'PASSED: N/N final blocking assertions' summary line (count is proof-of-work evidence; matches between numerator and denominator)
Frontmatter has status: verified
Frontmatter has build_target: python-script
Trigger condition for the feedback loop is explicit (e.g. user moves file post-routing, or user flags mis-route)
Output describes an updated routing rules artifact (file, table, or PR), not a re-routed file
Procedure has at least one decision point that distinguishes 'flaky route, no rule change needed' from 'systemic miss, rule should change'
Mermaid block has at least 5 step nodes plus at least one GATE decision node
Edge Cases include conflicting signals (two corrections disagree) or equivalent
Build handoff names Python-specific capture mechanisms (decorators, context managers, structured logging)
Decision rules are testable on input alone
Session notes describe each step that ran (Steps 1–7 always; Steps 0 and 8 only if their conditional triggers fired)
